# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Quote → Scope items UI redesign (V2 — RESUMABLE)

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-28.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## CRITICAL: This prompt is resumable across sessions

If a prior session ran this prompt and was terminated mid-flight (session limit, network drop, etc.), DO NOT start over. Pick up where it left off. The resume protocol is in Phase 0. Follow it exactly.

## Context — what's already shipped vs. what's left

PR #250 (just merged) already shipped the **data layer** for this feature:

- Schema: `QuoteScopeItem.quoteDiscipline` (nullable string) and `QuoteCostLine.displayDescription` (nullable string) exist in the DB and Prisma client.
- API: PATCH endpoints accept these fields.
- PDF: scope rows are grouped by `quoteDiscipline` (fallback to derived from wbsCode) and cost lines use `displayDescription` if set.

What this PR adds is the **React UI** to drive that backend:

1. Detailed-mode scope-items sub-tab: discipline-grouped table, per-discipline `+ Add row`, top-level `+ Add discipline` modal picker, label column read-only, Source column removed, DnD between disciplines (wbsCode stays stable on drag).
2. Simple-mode cost summary: editable description per row, persists to `displayDescription`.

## Operating rules

1. **Work in 6 phases.** Each phase ends in a single commit AND a push. Each commit message uses a distinctive prefix `[pr9-phase-N]` so a future session can detect progress via `git log`.
2. **After each phase, run `pnpm build` and `pnpm lint`.** Both must pass before the commit.
3. **Hard ABORT rule:** if you read/re-read the same file 3 times in a row without a successful Edit (whitespace mismatch), STOP and report. Do not loop.
4. **Stay on ONE branch the entire PR:** `feat/quote-scope-items-ui-v2`. Do not check out other branches.
5. **Each commit pushed before next phase.**

## Phase 0 — Resume detection + branch setup

**Step 0.1 — Determine resume state.** Run:

```
git fetch origin
git log --all --oneline | grep -E "\[pr9-phase-" | head -10
```

Parse the output. Look for the highest phase number in the messages. Examples:
- No matches → starting from scratch, go to Phase 1
- Latest commit message contains `[pr9-phase-1]` → start at Phase 2
- Latest contains `[pr9-phase-5]` → start at Phase 6
- All six phases done → just open the PR

**Step 0.2 — Branch hygiene.**

If NO `[pr9-phase-*]` commits exist anywhere:
```
git checkout main
git pull
git checkout -b feat/quote-scope-items-ui-v2
```

If `[pr9-phase-*]` commits exist on `feat/quote-scope-items-ui-v2`:
```
git checkout feat/quote-scope-items-ui-v2
git pull
```

Confirm working tree is clean. If not, stash with a unique message and report.

**Step 0.3 — Announce starting phase.** Tell the user: "Resuming PR-9 UI from Phase N" (or "Starting PR-9 UI from scratch").

## Phase 1 — Make label column read-only + remove Source column

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr9-phase-1\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Two tiny visual changes in the existing scope-items sub-tab table. No structural refactor yet.

1. Find the scope-items sub-tab table in the Quote → Edit view. Search `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/ClientQuotesPanel.tsx` for the columns `Label / Description / Qty / Unit / Notes / Source` (one of these is unique enough to anchor — search for `Source` as a column header).
2. **Label cell:** today renders as an `<input>`. Change to plain text rendering (`<div>` or `<span>` showing the wbsCode value). NOT editable.
3. **Source column:** drop the column entirely — remove from the header row, remove the cell in each row.
4. Run build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr9-phase-1] feat: label column read-only + remove Source column"
   git push -u origin feat/quote-scope-items-ui-v2
   ```

---

## Phase 2 — Render discipline-grouped table (replace flat list)

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr9-phase-2\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** The flat table becomes one section per discipline. Compute the discipline for each item as `item.quoteDiscipline ?? deriveFromWbsCode(item.wbsCode)`. Group items by that derived value.

1. Compute a `disciplineForItem(item)` helper that returns `item.quoteDiscipline` if set, otherwise the first 3 chars of `wbsCode` mapped to a discipline code (DEM, CIV, ASB, Other).
2. Build a memoized grouped map: `Record<discipline, Item[]>`.
3. Render section per discipline: heading `{disciplineName} ({count})`, table inside.
4. Use the existing `DISCIPLINE_LABELS` map from `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/scope-cards/utils/card-display.ts`.
5. Each section's table reuses the existing row rendering (just iterated within the section instead of the flat list).
6. Run build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr9-phase-2] feat: render scope items grouped by discipline"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 3 — Per-discipline "+ Add row" + top-level "+ Add discipline" modal

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr9-phase-3\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Add two new actions:

1. **Per-discipline `+ Add row`** below each discipline section heading. Click → adds a blank scope item to that discipline (wbsCode auto-generated using the discipline prefix and next sequential number; `quoteDiscipline` set to that discipline). Use the existing `POST /scope-items` endpoint with `quoteDiscipline` set.
2. **Top-level `+ Add discipline`** button below the table. Click opens a modal picker (similar to `NewCardModal.tsx` from PR #248). Pick a discipline → creates an empty group (no items yet — the group appears once an item exists).

   For the "empty group" UX: if the user picks a discipline that already has items, just scroll to it. If picking a new discipline (e.g. nothing exists yet for ASB), the group needs to render even with 0 items so the `+ Add row` is clickable. Maintain a local state `Set<string>` of "explicitly added empty groups" that persists for the session, rendered alongside groups derived from items.

3. **Remove the old top-level `+ Add item` button** that was a flat-create.
4. Run build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr9-phase-3] feat: per-discipline Add row + top-level Add discipline modal"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 4 — DnD library + sortable within each discipline

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr9-phase-4\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Add `@dnd-kit/core` + `@dnd-kit/sortable` if not already installed, wire sortable reordering WITHIN each discipline section (not cross-discipline yet).

1. Check `package.json` — search the codebase for existing `@dnd-kit` usage. If present already, reuse. If not:
   ```
   pnpm --filter @project-ops/web add @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/sortable
   ```
2. Wrap each discipline section in a `<DndContext>` with `<SortableContext>`. Each row becomes a sortable item.
3. Add a drag handle icon (⋮ or grip) at the start of each row.
4. On reorder within a discipline, persist via the existing reorder endpoint if there is one (search for `reorder` in the scope-items routes). If no reorder endpoint, accept order is visual-only for this phase and add a TODO.
5. Run build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr9-phase-4] feat: DnD reorder within discipline groups"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 5 — Cross-discipline DnD (set quoteDiscipline on drop)

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr9-phase-5\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** Allow dragging a row from one discipline section onto another. wbsCode stays the same — only `quoteDiscipline` updates via PATCH.

1. Wrap the WHOLE table (all discipline sections) in a single `<DndContext>` with handler for cross-section drops.
2. Each discipline section heading becomes a droppable zone.
3. On drop into a different section: PATCH the item with `{ quoteDiscipline: <new discipline code> }`. The wbsCode is unchanged — the item visually moves to the new section but its label stays e.g. `Civ1.5` even when it's now under Demolition.
4. Local state update on success so the UI moves the row immediately (optimistic) — revert on PATCH failure.
5. Run build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr9-phase-5] feat: cross-discipline DnD with stable wbsCode"
   git push
   ```

---

## Phase 6 — Simple-mode cost summary editable description

**Already done if:** `git log --oneline | grep -q "\[pr9-phase-6\]"` returns true.

**Scope:** In Simple mode (`Quote detail level === "Simple"`), the cost summary table's DESCRIPTION column becomes editable per row. Edits persist to `displayDescription` on the cost line.

1. Find the cost summary table render. It's likely in `ClientQuotesPanel.tsx` near the description for cost lines. Search for the column header `DESCRIPTION` or `Description` near `AMOUNT (EX GST)`.
2. In edit mode AND when `quote.detailLevel === "Simple"`, replace the description cell `<div>` with an `<input>` bound to `costLine.displayDescription ?? costLine.description`.
3. On blur, PATCH the cost line with `{ displayDescription: <input value> }` via the existing endpoint.
4. The SCOPE column (DEM, CIV, ASB, Other, Cutting) remains read-only.
5. Run build + lint. Commit:
   ```
   git add -A
   git commit -m "[pr9-phase-6] feat: simple-mode editable cost summary descriptions"
   git push
   ```

## Final step — Open the PR

**Already done if:** a PR exists on GitHub for branch `feat/quote-scope-items-ui-v2`.

Once all 6 phase commits are on origin:

- Open PR via `gh pr create` or whichever CLI is set up.
- Title: `[5A] Quote → Scope items UI redesign (discipline grouping + DnD + simple-mode rename)`
- Body: list the six phases with one-line summaries. Note that the data layer (schema/API/PDF) was already shipped by PR #250 — this PR is purely the React UI on top.
- Reviewer `GH-Mantova`.
- **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies live.

## Tests

Light per-phase coverage:

- Phase 1: label cell renders as non-input; Source column absent.
- Phase 2: items grouped by discipline; counts correct.
- Phase 3: + Add row PATCHes with correct discipline; + Add discipline modal renders four options.
- Phase 4-5: drag a row, confirm PATCH fires with correct `quoteDiscipline`.
- Phase 6: simple-mode description input PATCHes `displayDescription`.

## Scope — do NOT do

- Do NOT touch the data layer (schema, API, migrations) — already shipped.
- Do NOT change the wbsCode generation logic — `quoteDiscipline` is independent of wbsCode.
- Do NOT touch the Provisional Sums / Cost Options / Assumptions / Exclusions / T&C tabs (separate concerns).
- Do NOT make a single Edit call that rewrites >100 lines. Split into smaller edits.

## If you get stuck

Stop and report:
- Which phase you're on.
- The file's current state (`git diff HEAD --stat`).
- The error or whitespace mismatch.
- What you've tried.

DO NOT loop on the same Edit call. DO NOT reason about "external processes reverting edits" — whitespace mismatch in `old_string`, every time.
